title: Inline image insertion in blog posts, remove login page, fix missing post in admin

requirements.text: In the blog post create and edit forms (CreateBlogPostPage.tsx and EditBlogPostPage.tsx), add an 'Insert Image' toolbar button within the content editor area. When clicked, it should allow the user to upload an image and insert an inline image placeholder/tag at the current cursor position in the content text area. The inserted image must render correctly in BlogPostDetailPage.tsx when the post is displayed.

quotes: provide option to include image in-between main content of my blog post

acceptanceCriteria: An 'Insert Image' button is visible in the content editor toolbar on both CreateBlogPostPage and EditBlogPostPage.
Clicking the button opens a file picker or upload dialog for selecting an image.
The selected image is uploaded and an inline image tag/placeholder is inserted at the current cursor position in the content textarea.
When the blog post is viewed on BlogPostDetailPage, inline images appear correctly at the positions they were inserted.
Existing inline image rendering logic in BlogPostDetailPage and imageUtils.ts is preserved and extended to support new insertions.

requirements.text: Remove the login/authentication gate from the admin upload flow. The admin page for creating and uploading blog posts (CreateBlogPostPage.tsx and AdminPostsPage.tsx) should be directly accessible without requiring Internet Identity login — i.e., render it as a simple open admin page like it was before any login requirement was added. Remove or bypass any AdminRoute guard or authentication check that currently blocks access to these pages.

quotes: remove login page by making normal admin page to upload blog post just like earlier

acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to the admin page (e.g., /admin or /admin/posts) does not redirect to a login page or show an access-denied screen.
The AdminRoute guard is removed or set to always allow access for admin-facing pages.
The create/edit/delete blog post functionality remains fully operational without authentication.
Internet Identity login/logout in the Navigation bar can remain for other purposes but is not required to access admin pages.

requirements.text: Fix the bug where the blog post titled 'Best Ayurvedic Herbs for Glowing Skin' does not appear on the admin page (AdminPostsPage.tsx). Investigate whether the issue is caused by a data fetch problem, a publish/draft state filter, or a backend query issue, and ensure all existing blog posts — regardless of publish state — are listed on the admin page.

quotes: the blog post you just tried to add ("Best Ayurvedic Herbs for Glowing Skin") this blog post is not showing admin page

acceptanceCriteria: The 'Best Ayurvedic Herbs for Glowing Skin' blog post is visible in the admin posts list.
All blog posts, including unpublished/draft ones, are shown on the admin page.
The backend query used by AdminPostsPage fetches all posts without filtering out drafts or unpublished posts.
No console errors related to missing posts appear when the admin page loads.

constraints: Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
Preserve all existing inline image rendering logic in BlogPostDetailPage.tsx and imageUtils.ts when extending for new inline image insertion.
Do not break existing blog post CRUD operations when removing the authentication gate.
nonGoals: Adding a full rich-text (WYSIWYG) editor — a simple toolbar button for image insertion within the existing textarea is sufficient.
Implementing any new authentication system or replacing Internet Identity.
Redesigning the visual theme or layout of any pages not directly related to the three requirements above.
